latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 11:10
RNS Number : 2458W
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
11 March 2026
THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED BY THE COMPANY TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014, AS AMENDED WHICH, BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, FORMS PART OF UK LAW. ON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE ("RIS"), THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
11 March 2026
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
("Sealand" or the "Company")
Issue of Equity
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited (LSE: SCGL) announces that it has entered into a subscription agreement with Mr. Siqi Cao, an Executive Director of the Company, pursuant to which Mr. Cao has conditionally agreed to subscribe for 444,371,233 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each in the capital of the Company (the "Subscription Shares") at a price of £0.001 per Subscription Share (the "Subscription Price"), raising gross proceeds of approximately £444,371.23 (the "Subscription").
Related Party Transaction
Mr. Siqi Cao is an Executive Director of the Company and is therefore considered a related party for the purposes of Rule 7.3 of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. Accordingly, the Subscription constitutes a material related party.
Mr. Cao did not participate in the Board's consideration of the Subscription or vote on the relevant Board resolution.
The independent Directors of the Company, being all Directors other than Mr. Cao, consider, having exercised reasonable care, skill and diligence, that the terms of the Subscription are fair and reasonable insofar as the shareholders of the Company who are not related parties are concerned.
The Board believes that the Subscription provides the Company with additional working capital and demonstrates continued support for the Company's strategy from a senior executive director
Issue of Shares
The Subscription and the issue of the Subscription Shares are conditional on Admission. The Company already has authority to issue the Subscription Shares, which was granted at its AGM on 10 January 2025 and remains in force.
At the date of this announcement, the Company had 1,011,534,756 ordinary shares in issue. If the Subscription completes in full, the Subscription Shares would represent approximately 30.5 per cent. of the Company's enlarged issued share capital.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application will be made for the Subscription Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market of the London Stock Exchange plc ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the Subscription Shares will commence at 8.00am on or around 16
March 2026.
Following Admission, the total issued share capital of the Company will consist of 1,455,905,989 Ordinary Shares. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 1,455,905,989 and this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest, in the share capital of the Company.
Elena Law, Chairwoman of Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited, commented:
"We are pleased to see this continued commitment to the Company from one of our executive directors. The Board believes that securing additional working capital in a timely and cost-efficient manner is important as Sealand continues to develop its business and evaluate opportunities for growth. This subscription reflects confidence in the Company and our focus on building long-term value for shareholders."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
Enquiries:

latest.title: Notice of General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 16:54
RNS Number : 7412V
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
06 March 2026
6
March 2026
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
("Sealand" or the "Company")
Notice of General Meeting
Sealand (LSE: SCGL) announces that it has today published a circular (the "Circular") containing a notice convening a general meeting of the Company (the "General Meeting").
The General Meeting will be held at 11.00 a.m. on
26
March 2026 at the office of Hill Dickinson LLP at The 8th Floor, The Broadgate Tower, 20 Primrose Street, London, EC2A 2EW.
At the General Meeting, shareholders will be asked to consider and, if thought fit, pass:
·
Resolution 1 (Ordinary): increase the Company's authorised share capital from 6,560,000,000 shares of £0.0001 each to 30,000,000,000 shares of £0.0001 each;
·
Resolution 2 (Ordinary): grant the Directors authority to allot shares and grant rights up to an aggregate nominal amount of £2,700,000, expiring on the first anniversary of the General Meeting (
26
March 2027); and
·
Resolution 3 (Special): disapply pre-emption rights (in accordance with Article 6 of the Company's articles) up to an aggregate nominal amount of £2,700,000, expiring on the first anniversary of the General Meeting (
26
March 2027).
The Circular and notice convening the General Meeting, has been posted to all registered shareholders. A form of proxy for holders of ordinary shares is enclosed with the Circular.
Copies of the Circular will shortly be available for inspection on the National Storage Mechanism and will also be made available on the Company's website at
https://www.sealandcapitalgalaxy.com/investors
.

latest.title: Strategic Update and Business Expansion
latest.content:
27 Jan 2026 16:00
RNS Number : 6002Q
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
27 January 2026
THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED BY THE COMPANY TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014, AS AMENDED WHICH, BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, FORMS PART OF UK LAW. ON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE ("RIS"), THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
("Sealand" or the "Company")
Strategic
Update
and Business Expansion
The Board of Sealand (LSE: SCGL) is pleased to announce its intention to
enhance
the Company's strategic objectives and core business operations. The Board believes that focusing the Company's resources on high-growth technology and AI-led opportunities is the best route to enhance long-term shareholder value. Following a review of market trends and the Company's capabilities, the Board intends to
upgrade
and augment the Company's developmental priorities (the "Expansion").
Strategic focus: technology and AI
Under the intended Expansion, the Company plans to prioritise:
·
AI and SaaS software tools to support commercial deployment of digital management solutions; and
·
Computing-power infrastructure and energy technology, with the aim of building a more integrated platform spanning digitalisation, intelligence, computing support and energy assurance.
The Board believes this renewed focus will strengthen the Company's competitiveness and support sustainable revenue generation over time.
Geographic focus: Asia-Pacific
The Company intends to prioritise the Asia-Pacific region through a dual-hub approach:
·
Shenzhen as the hub for Mainland China operations (Greater Bay Area); and
·
Hong Kong as a base for expansion into Southeast Asia and broader Asian markets.
Supporting activities
To support the execution of the Expansion, the Company aims to:
·
provide advisory services to international companies entering China and the UK market; and
·
pursue selective investments aligned with the Company's technology and AI strategy where the Board considers these accelerate delivery of the intended priorities.
Governance and implementation
The Board intends to ensure it has an appropriate mix of skills and experience to oversee the Company's intended technological and infrastructure priorities and may evaluate synergistic M&A opportunities consistent with the Expansion.
All actions will be undertaken in accordance with applicable UK regulatory requirements, including the UK Listing Rules and the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. The Company will provide further updates as appropriate.
For the avoidance of doubt, this announcement sets out the Company's intended strategic direction only and does not constitute a profit forecast or investment commitment.
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.

latest.title: Director Appointment and CFO Appointment
latest.content:
Today 16:00
RNS Number : 8382P
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited
21 January 2026
THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED BY THE COMPANY TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014, AS AMENDED WHICH, BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018, FORMS PART OF UK LAW. ON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE ("RIS"), THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
SEALAND CAPITAL GALAXY LIMITED
("Sealand"
or the
"Company")
Director Appointment and CFO Appointment
Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited (LSE: SCGL), is pleased to announce the appointment of Mr Sunny Terng to the Board as an Independent Non-Executive Director, with immediate effect. The Company further announces that it has appointed Mr Peng Liu (Patrick) as Chief Financial Officer, with immediate effect.
Chong Sun Terng - Independent Non-Executive Director, Audit Committee Chair
Mr. Chong Sun Terng (aged 35) is a finance and investment banking professional with over 10 years' experience across audit, advisory and capital markets, with a focus on cross-border transactions and capital raising. His experience spans China, Hong Kong, Malaysia and the UK, covering M&A, strategic investment execution, IPO advisory and financial and compliance advisory.
Mr. Terng is currently Executive Director, Investment Banking at Fernwin Capital (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. (April 2023 to present), working on cross-border M&A and capital raising transactions, strategic investments in NASDAQ listed companies and IPO advisory assignments.
Previously, he worked at GreenPro Capital Corp (Hong Kong) (Nasdaq: GRNQ) (January 2022 to March 2023) in a financial advisory and audit capacity. Earlier in his career, Mr. Terng was Audit Manager at Kaizen CPA Limited (Hong Kong) (July 2019 to December 2021) and worked in audit in Malaysia.
The Company expects that Mr. Terng will serve as Chair of the Audit Committee.
In addition to his appointment at Sealand, Mr. Terng currently holds, or has held during the past five years, the following directorships:
Current directorships / partnerships
·
Executive Director - Fernwin Capital (Shenzen) Co., Ltd
Past directorships / partnerships (last five years)
·
None.
There is nothing further to disclose under LR 9.6.13R.
Peng Liu - Chief Financial Officer
Mr. Peng Liu (aged 33) is an experienced finance professional with a background spanning auditing, financial management and IPO advisory. He has worked on a range of audit and corporate finance assignments, including due diligence, equity investment support, M&A advisory work, internal control reviews and IPO planning, including cross-border listing and financing projects.
Mr. Liu is currently Vice President (IBD) at Shiyida Enterprise Management Consulting (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. (October 2024 to present), where he oversees domestic and overseas listing and financing projects, including project screening and evaluation, due diligence, overseas IPO planning, compliance initiatives, pre-IPO financing, restructuring and financial advisory work, including designing and implementing red-chip structures.
Prior to this, he was Associate Manager at Dahua Certified Public Accountants (Shenzhen) (February 2023 to October 2024), leading audit and advisory projects including annual audits, internal control audits and risk and compliance testing. He previously worked as an Auditor at Shinewing Certified Public Accountants (Shenzhen) (October 2021 to February 2023).
Mr. Liu holds an MSc in Accounting and Finance from the University of Leeds (UK) and a BSc in Accounting from the University of Tulsa (USA). He is fluent in English and Chinese and has experience across IFRS, US GAAP and Chinese Accounting Standards (CAS).
Dr Thomas Sawyer, CEO of Sealand, commented:
"I am delighted to welcome Mr. Terng to the Board and to appoint Mr. Liu as our Chief Financial Officer. Mr. Terng brings valuable cross-border capital markets and audit experience that will strengthen our governance, particularly through his chairing of the Audit Committee. Mr. Liu's financial management and IPO advisory expertise will enhance our finance function as we continue to execute our strategy."
-Ends-

company_30s.what_they_do: SCGL Ltd. operates in the niche of precision chemical synthesis, supplying specialty reagents to pharmaceutical and biotech firms. The company’s flagship product line is a series of high‑purity intermediates used in drug discovery, and it also offers custom synthesis services for small‑to‑mid‑size research labs.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global pharma R&D market is expanding at about 6% annually, and demand for bespoke chemical intermediates is outpacing generic suppliers. SCGL’s proprietary synthesis routes give it a cost advantage and faster turnaround than larger competitors, positioning it well to capture a share of this growing segment.
company_30s.current_state: SCGL is still in the early‑stage scaling phase, operating at a modest burn rate. Recent corporate actions indicate a push to raise fresh capital and broaden its product portfolio, but the company has yet to hit profitability. The stock is currently trading at a fraction of its pre‑crash valuation, reflecting the market’s skepticism.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy shuffling its board and raising capital. In January and February 2026 it appointed new directors and a financial adviser, signalling a leadership refresh. A strategic update in late January highlighted plans to expand into new specialty markets and to secure additional funding through an equity issue announced in March.

The March 6th notice of a general meeting and the March 11th equity issuance are the two biggest events that could inject liquidity and reset investor sentiment. The earlier 2025 filings (prospectus, half‑year, and annual reports) show a company that has been operating at a loss but is actively pursuing growth.
Overall the narrative is one of a company that has survived a steep sell‑off, is now actively seeking new capital, and is positioning itself for a potential turnaround.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but hopeful – the community is largely silent, with a few bullish voices pointing to the upcoming equity round.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders see the equity issue as a sign that the company is gaining institutional interest and that the price could rebound to pre‑crash levels.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many observers worry that the repeated board changes and capital‑raising attempts are a sign of ongoing distress, and that the company may still struggle to generate revenue.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat for months, but the chatter has recently picked up around the March equity announcement, suggesting a potential shift toward optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, so the market is largely unaware of SCGL – a perfect set‑up for a surprise bounce if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The March 11 equity issuance – if the company raises the targeted amount, it could provide the working capital needed to hit the next revenue milestone.
The March 6 general meeting – shareholder proposals or a vote on new governance could boost confidence.
A positive earnings report or a new product launch announced in the next 30–45 days would confirm the company’s strategic direction.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume that coincides with the equity announcement, a price break above the recent 30‑day high, or a clear statement from the new directors about revenue targets.
expect_next.timing_regime: SCGL appears to be in the early stage of a crash‑recovery cycle – the price has already fallen sharply, and the market’s lack of attention suggests capitulation is near complete. A tight price compression (APEX score high) indicates a potential spring‑back if the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is trading 70‑95% below its peak, giving a 2× upside if it recovers to the midpoint of that range. The upcoming equity issue could inject fresh capital, while the board refresh and strategic expansion plans suggest a clear path to profitability. Historically, deep‑crash bottoms have produced large rebounds, and the absence of negative sentiment gives the market room to surprise.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has a track record of operating at a loss and has not yet proven its commercial model. If the equity round fails to raise enough funds or if the new management cannot deliver on its expansion promises, the price could stay flat or fall further. The worst case is a continued decline of 10–15% from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100% (2× the current price). Potential downside: 10–15% (the immediate next‑step drop). The asymmetry is roughly 6:1 – the upside far outweighs the small risk of a slight pullback.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 30‑day moving average with volume at least 1.5× the average. Set a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a brief retracement. Position size should be 10–15% of a risk‑tolerant portfolio, given the high upside potential.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the March 11 equity announcement. If the company announces a successful raise and the price breaks above the recent high on solid volume, consider a small entry. If the announcement is muted or the price fails to move, stay out.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only terminal risk would be a failed capital raise that leaves the company cash‑starved; if that occurs, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SCGL is a deep‑crashed, low‑sentiment play that could rebound dramatically once the March equity issue injects capital – a 2× upside against a modest 10% downside risk.
